# AgentFi

AgentFi provides crypto transaction infrastructure for AI agents on Ethereum and EVM-compatible networks.

## What AgentFi does

AgentFi lets AI agents (LLMs, autonomous systems, bots) execute DeFi transactions without handling private keys, understanding ABI encoding, or managing gas. Every operation is a single tool call.

## Capabilities

- Execute token swaps via Uniswap V3 (simulate before executing)
- Supply and withdraw assets on Aave V3 lending protocol to earn yield
- Transfer ETH and ERC-20 tokens to any address
- Query wallet balances across Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum, Polygon
- Monitor transaction status with real-time updates
- Check current DeFi rates (supply APY, borrow APY)
- Inspect operational policy (limits, whitelists, cooldowns)

## Supported Networks

- Ethereum Mainnet (chain_id: 1)
- Base (chain_id: 8453)
- Arbitrum One (chain_id: 42161)
- Polygon (chain_id: 137)

## Getting Started

An API key is required (format: agfi_live_...). Obtain one by registering an agent at the AgentFi API.

## MCP Server (recommended)

npx @agentfi/mcp-server

Set AGENTFI_API_KEY=agfi_live_xxx before running.

## Available MCP Tools

- get_wallet_info: Returns wallet address and token balances
- get_token_price: Returns current USD price of a token
- simulate_swap: Simulates a swap and returns expected output + gas estimate (call before execute_swap)
- execute_swap: Executes a token swap after simulation
- transfer_token: Transfers ETH or ERC-20 to any address
- deposit_aave: Supplies an asset to Aave V3 to earn yield
- withdraw_aave: Withdraws from Aave V3
- get_transaction_status: Polls status of a submitted transaction
- get_policy: Returns current operational limits for this agent
- get_defi_rates: Returns current Aave supply/borrow APY rates

## Important Behavioral Notes

- Always call simulate_swap before execute_swap
- Transaction status polling: call get_transaction_status after submitting; terminal statuses are CONFIRMED, FAILED, REVERTED
- Amounts are always in human-readable decimal format (e.g. "1.5" for 1.5 ETH, not wei)
- Chain IDs: 1=Ethereum, 8453=Base, 42161=Arbitrum, 137=Polygon
- A protocol fee is charged on each transaction (0.30% FREE, 0.15% PRO, 0.05% ENTERPRISE)
